methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $26M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Fuse Energy

data.narrative:
Deep Isolation is a Series A nuclear energy company founded by Elizabeth Muller that has raised $26M to date. The valuation universe comprises 11 comparable companies: 3 public peers (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private nuclear energy firms including Fuse Energy, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power.

Stage-progression analysis is the applicable triangulation method here. Using total raised ($26M) as a proxy for current valuation and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement to the next financing stage yields an implied valuation of $52M. A peer-median approach could not be applied; while 8 same-sector comparables exist in the broader nuclear energy space, the narrow filter (same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar valuation tier) produced insufficient datapoints—fewer than the three required for reliable median estimation—making cross-sector extrapolation unreliable.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $44M to $60M, with a median point of $52M. This range reflects the single applicable method and inherent uncertainty around stage-progression multiples in the nascent nuclear technology sector. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the 2.0x multiplier for Deep Isolation's specific risk profile and capital efficiency relative to peer financing trajectories, as well as the broader market appetite for Series B deployment in advanced nuclear ventures. Additional comparable data or transaction evidence from the narrowly-defined peer cohort would strengthen conviction in this range.
